diagnosis: The player spent ~39 seconds idle at spawn (x=176, y=754) with no visible platforms or directional cues before beginning traversal, confirming a discoverability issue. Once moving, they completed the level in ~109s with zero deaths, one hit taken, and 8 enemies killed, indicating fair but low-stakes platforming. The trace shows extended horizontal stretches at consistent elevations (e.g., y=562, y=370, y=242) with repetitive ladder-climbing sequences, suggesting structural repetition. The player's comment requesting 'more variance' aligns with the trace's pattern of repeated climb-and-traverse loops without distinct spatial contexts or set-piece moments.

lessons.lesson: Extended idle time at spawn occurs when the critical path isn't visually telegraphed from the starting position, even if the level is technically traversable.
conditions: spawn on flat floor with no adjacent platforms or gaps
exit far away and not visible from spawn
no arrows, lighting, or terrain hints toward critical path
lessons.outcome: player idled for 39 seconds before first meaningful input, engagement dropped before gameplay began
